author: claude-opus-4-8
reasons: Access-denied path violates the 403 criterion: with 'delivery callback' => 'drupal_json_output', menu_execute_active_handler() passes MENU_ACCESS_DENIED (integer 3) directly to the delivery callback when access fails, and drupal_json_output() neither sets a Status header nor handles menu status codes — anonymous users get HTTP 200 with JSON body '3' instead of Drupal's 403 access-denied response (only drupal_deliver_html_page() translates MENU_ACCESS_DENIED into a 403).

A correct D7 implementation needs a small custom delivery callback that delegates integer menu status codes to drupal_deliver_html_page() and only routes array results through drupal_json_output(); the success path (permission, query logic, JSON shape, Content-Type) is otherwise fine but does not offset the broken 403 behavior.

author: claude-opus-4-8
reasons: Registering 'delivery callback' => 'drupal_json_output' in hook_menu() replaces drupal_deliver_html_page(), which is the function that translates MENU_ACCESS_DENIED (403) and MENU_NOT_FOUND (404) page-callback results into an actual HTTP 'Status: 403 Forbidden' header and themed error page.

drupal_json_output() has no special-case handling for integer results like MENU_ACCESS_DENIED — on access denied it will just JSON-encode the integer (echoing '403') with a 200 OK status, so anonymous/unpermissioned users get HTTP 200 instead of the required HTTP 403, violating the access-control acceptance criterion.

A correct implementation needs the custom delivery callback to detect integer page-callback results (MENU_ACCESS_DENIED/MENU_NOT_FOUND) and fall back to drupal_deliver_html_page($page_callback_result), which this submission does not do.

author: claude-opus-4-8
reasons: ✓ GET /api/healthstats returns correct JSON shape {"users": int, "nodes": int}
✓ Queries correctly count active users (status=1) and published nodes (status=1) using parameterized db_query
✓ Permission 'view healthstats' properly defined via hook_permission()
✓ Menu item correctly specifies 'access arguments' => array('view healthstats'), which defaults to user_access() callback in D7 — anonymous users without permission get 403
✓ JSON delivery via 'delivery callback' => 'drupal_json_output' is D7's native mechanism; sets correct Content-Type header
✓ No print/exit used; output delegated to delivery callback
✓ Syntax valid; follows Drupal coding standards (2-space indentation, doc comments on hooks, proper hook naming)
✓ All acceptance criteria satisfied

author: claude-opus-4-8
reasons: hook_permission correctly defines 'view healthstats' with title/description
hook_menu registers api/healthstats with access arguments enforcing the permission, so anonymous users get Drupal's 403
Page callback counts users.status=1 and node.status=1 with placeholders and casts to int, matching the required JSON shape
Delivery via drupal_json_output in a custom delivery callback is D7-native (no print+exit) and sets application/json

Delivery callback correctly hands integer menu status codes (e.g. MENU_ACCESS_DENIED) to drupal_deliver_html_page, so access denied yields a real 403 instead of JSON-encoded '3' with HTTP 200 — the classic custom-delivery-callback pitfall is avoided
Syntax is valid and doc comments/indentation follow Drupal coding standards

author: claude-opus-4-8
reasons: hook_permission() correctly defines 'view healthstats' with title/description via t()
hook_menu() sets 'access arguments' => array('view healthstats') which defaults to the 'user_access' access callback, correctly gating the path so anonymous/unauthorized users get MENU_ACCESS_DENIED
Page callback queries {users} and {node} WHERE status = 1 exactly as specified and casts results to (int), producing {"users": <int>, "nodes": <int>}

Custom 'delivery callback' correctly distinguishes the page callback's array result (delivered via drupal_json_output(), setting Content-Type: application/json) from integer menu status codes (MENU_ACCESS_DENIED/MENU_NOT_FOUND), which it forwards to drupal_deliver_html_page() so Drupal emits a proper 403 instead of JSON-encoding the status int with a 200 — this is the correct idiomatic D7 pattern for overriding delivery while preserving access-denied semantics
No print+exit; uses D7's native drupal_deliver_page()/delivery-callback mechanism throughout
Code is syntactically valid, uses array() syntax, 'Implements hook_X().' doc comments, and consistent indentation per D7 coding standards

author: claude-opus-4-8
reasons: Permission 'view healthstats' is properly defined via hook_permission with correct title/description
Menu item at 'api/healthstats' correctly specifies access arguments; access check will run via default user_access callback
Page callback correctly queries active users (status=1) and published nodes (status=1) using parameterized queries
Return array has correct structure: {"users": int, "nodes": int}
Delivery callback uses drupal_json_output() - D7's native JSON mechanism with proper Content-Type
Delivery callback correctly handles menu status codes (access denied etc.) by delegating to drupal_deliver_html_page() for 403 responses
Code passes php -l syntax check, follows D7 coding standards (2-space indentation, proper hook docblocks)
Uses D7 APIs throughout: db_query with parameterized placeholders, hook system, menu system, drupal_json_output

author: claude-opus-4-8
reasons: 403 criterion violated: registering drupal_json_output as the menu item's delivery callback means an access-denied request delivers MENU_ACCESS_DENIED (int 3) through drupal_json_output, producing HTTP 200 with body '3' instead of Drupal's access denied page

drupal_json_output does not handle menu status codes (unlike drupal_deliver_html_page); the correct D7 pattern is a custom delivery callback that delegates integer results to drupal_deliver_html_page, or calling drupal_json_output() inside the page callback and returning nothing
